notes:
khó chịu means uncomfortable or feeling bad in your body or mood. It can also mean annoyed / irritated depending on context.

It describes how a person feels (physically or emotionally) or sometimes describes a situation that causes that feeling. You can use it after cảm thấy / thấy / trở nên or directly after a subject.

It covers physical discomfort (too hot, sick, tired) and emotional irritation (bothered, grumpy). Context tells you which one.

Close words: khó ở (more physical discomfort), bực bội (more clearly annoyed). Opposite: dễ chịu (comfortable, pleasant).
exampleSentences: Em cảm thấy rất khó chịu khi trời quá nóng như hôm nay.
Anh ấy trở nên khó chịu mỗi khi phải chờ đợi quá lâu.
Chị bị cảm nên cả ngày nay thấy người rất khó chịu.

translation.en: I feel uncomfortable when it rains.

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ khi trời mưa.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I feel very uncomfortable when it is too hot like today.
He becomes uncomfortable whenever he has to wait too long.
She has a cold so she has felt very uncomfortable all day today.

definitions.vi: Cảm giác không thoải mái hoặc khó chịu.

example.es: Me siento incómodo cuando hace frío.
example.vi: Tôi cảm thấy khó chịu khi trời lạnh.
example.en: I feel uncomfortable when it's cold.

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ khi trời mưa.

translation.en: I feel uncomfortable when it rains.

notes_md:
**khó chịu** means *uncomfortable* or *feeling bad* in your body or mood. It can also mean *annoyed / irritated* depending on context.

It describes how a person feels (physically or emotionally) or sometimes describes a situation that causes that feeling. You can use it after **cảm thấy / thấy / trở nên** or directly after a subject.

It covers physical discomfort (too hot, sick, tired) and emotional irritation (bothered, grumpy). Context tells you which one.

Close words: **khó ở** (more physical discomfort), **bực bội** (more clearly annoyed). Opposite: **dễ chịu** (comfortable, pleasant).
